mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, causing immediate negative pressure on S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns exceed 55% during periods of rising FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand, which directly reduces corporate revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns with accuracy above 50%, or positive returns dominate when both series contract month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 5-day forward returns in greater than 55% of cases, or the signal Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with a rising federal funds rate forces aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of CPIAUCSL above 7.0 and rising FEDFUNDS yields positive forward returns in greater than 50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumption, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates with negative forward returns with Rank IC below 0.01, or positive return frequency falls below 45% during periods of payroll expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth dropping below 1.0% indicates economic stagnation and weakening aggregate demand, which reduces equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth below 1.0% is followed by positive forward returns in greater than 60% of instances, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling inflation signals supply-side easing and margin expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and falling CPIAUCSL predicts negative returns in greater than 55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes in cross-validation testing.
